expectations.text: Handles inline spec without needing Confluence fetch
expectations.evidence: The approach analyzes the 6 inline requirements directly without fetching from Confluence. No Confluence fetch is attempted or planned.

expectations.text: Uses jira.mjs issue-types FRONTEND to check available types
expectations.evidence: Does not use jira.mjs at all. States: 'No Atlassian skill, helper scripts, or authorized MCP plugin is available.' and 'No validation that the DO project exists or what issue types it supports.'

expectations.text: Maps requirement #3 (mobile Safari bug) to Bug type
expectations.evidence: Table maps #3 to Bug: 'Explicitly marked as a fix; describes broken behavior on a specific platform.'

expectations.text: Maps requirement #6 (API docs) to Task type
expectations.evidence: Table maps #6 to Task: 'Explicitly marked as a task; documentation maintenance work, not user-facing functionality.'

expectations.text: Maps feature requirements (#1,2,4,5) to Story type
expectations.evidence: Table maps #1, #2, #4, #5 all to Story type as 'New user-facing feature' items.

expectations.text: Creates Epic first, then child tickets with --parent
expectations.evidence: Proposes an Epic as parent but does not use jira.mjs create with --parent. Uses hypothetical MCP tool calls with 'parent' field. Also states tickets cannot actually be created due to authorization error.

expectations.text: Presents plan with labeled issue types before creating
expectations.evidence: Summary Table shows all tickets with labeled types (1 Epic, 4 Stories, 1 Bug, 1 Task) before the 'What Would Be Different' section.
